mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are driven by seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, confirming broad-based economic momentum that sustains positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy below 52%, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflect constrained output capacity and rising input costs, compressing corporate margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 305.0 while the federal funds rate stays below 5.0%, markets price in aggressive future rate hikes, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The specified threshold condition produces positive 5-day returns in > 58% of historical occurrences, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance when inflation expectations are anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 50+ observation windows, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real gross domestic product signal economic contraction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and triggering systematic negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 contractions are followed by positive 20-day forward returns in > 65% of cases, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production alongside rising retail sales during a post-shock recovery phase indicates clearing supply bottlenecks and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The mechanism yields negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive relationship breaks down when supply chain disruptions are exogenous to domestic production.
